title: The Street of Many Doors
author: Barış Kısır
short_description: A woman walks the same Prague street each day, opens a different door, and steps into a different version of her life.
long_description: Elena arrives in Prague to translate a forgotten poet's work. She discovers a street lined with doors of every color, and when she opens each one she enters a life she might have lived. She is a mother, a pianist, a widow, a diplomat, a thief, a scientist, a refugee, a lover, a prisoner of grief, and a woman who has never left. Each life is as real as the one she left behind. Guided by a mysterious Map Seller and watched by a silent Man at a Cafe, Elena must walk through a year of doors to discover whether there is a true self beneath the choices, or whether every self is just a door we walked through.
main_characters: Elena — A translator in her early thirties, adrift after a loss she cannot name, who walks the Street of Many Doors and enters different versions of her life
The Map Seller — An old man who tends a tiny shop at the end of the street, selling maps of places that do not exist. He built the street and understands its purpose
The Man at the Cafe — A quiet presence who sits at the same outdoor table every afternoon. He is the last door, the one that leads to the life Elena is actually building
